config.name: SEO Expert
config.description: Helps you review your site's SEO performance and discover areas for improvement.
config.author: QuarkA

messages.0: Let's take a look at your SEO. What would you like to focus on?
messages.1100: Let's check the total number of visits and see what percentage comes from search engines.
messages.1101: About {$organic_rate}% of visits are from search engines.
messages.1200: Next, let's take a closer look at search engine traffic.
messages.1201:
Here are the top pages attracting traffic from search engines.
Are any of these pages leading users toward your goals?
If not, consider using heatmaps or session recordings to review the page in detail.
messages.1210:
A bounce rate of around 80% for search traffic is generally acceptable. Pages with bounce rates over 90% may need improvement.
Now extracting pages with over 90% bounce rate and at least 10 visits.
messages.1211:
We've extracted pages with over 90% bounce rate and more than 10 visits.
Use heatmaps to check for links or banners guiding users to other pages, and consider making improvements.
messages.91211: Currently, there are no high-traffic pages with high bounce rates that need attention. Keep up the good work and continue regular checks.
messages.1999: That wraps up the SEO report. If you'd like to find new keyword ideas or articles suitable for rewriting, please choose from the menu. You can also check Google's official SEO Starter Guide: SEO Starter Guide
messages.2000:
Targeting keywords similar to those that have recently improved in ranking can strengthen your site's theme and help increase traffic.
Extracting trending keywords.
messages.2100:
Let's review trends in rising keywords.
We'll also go over how to discover keyword suggestions based on these trends.
messages.2110:
[How to Discover Keyword Suggestions]
1. Identify potential keywords
- For proper nouns: Try combinations like "Proper noun + topic" (e.g., "iPad + price", "iPad + usage"). Niche content with low competition tends to rank more easily.
- For other cases: Review keyword trends and identify candidates relevant to your site's theme.
messages.2120:
2. Finalize your keyword list
Search your candidates on Google and review the top-ranking pages.
Try to create content that improves upon those pages.
As a rule of thumb, use one page per keyword, though adding headings to existing articles can also be effective.
messages.2999: Keep creating new content regularly based on your selected keywords.
messages.3000: Please choose from the options below.
messages.3100:
Here are candidate pages for title and meta description rewrites. Selection criteria:
- More than 10 visits per page
- Click-through rate is lower than average based on recent rankings
messages.8000: This feature is powered by AI analysis. Please wait for about a minute...
messages.92000:
Keyword extraction requires data from Google Search Console.
No data is currently available. If you haven't linked your Google account, please do so.
messages.93000:
Title rewrite suggestions require data from Google Search Console.
No data is currently available. Please connect your Google account if you haven't already.
messages.93100:
No rewrite candidates matching the criteria were found.
If you'd like to improve your rankings, try the "Find keyword suggestions" feature.
messages.99910: No data matching the conditions was found.
messages.99999: That concludes the SEO advice. Great job!
messages.1: This feature isn't available just yet, but it's coming soon. Integration with Google Search Console is planned for the official release.
messages.2: Thanks for being part of our beta journey. Exciting things are coming – see you soon!
